titleStmt.title: Letter from Seán Lehane to Thomas Daly, 20 November 1923
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Seán Lehane

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Sean Lehane, Scarth, Bantry to Tomas Dalaig (Thomas Daly) Knockane, Firies, County Kerry, 20 October 1924. Lehane is glad to hear that Thomas is free and that Charlie and his comrade's bodies are to be returned. He asks him to send him the funeral arrangements so that Charlie's Northern comrades can make it to the funeral. English with Irish salutation and valediction. One page and one envelope.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
P41/3/5/6 Icart
Bantry 20/10/21st A Thomáis, a chara Yours of Friday last to hand
glad to find you doing fine. I am glad also that the poor lads
bodies are to be handed back. I will communicate with the
lads here who were with me up
north & we will try & attend at the
re-burial. Please let me know
if you possibly can what arrangemts
you will make, so as to give us
a chance of being in time. Hoping this finds you & yours
in the best of form Do cara
Sean Lehane
